items.back: Apply radar separation to ALL RNAV aircraft operating at and below FL450 on Q routes or random RNAV routes, excluding oceanic airspace. Exception: point-to-point routes via impromptu route.

items.front: Four pairings where radar separation may be applied (5-5-1b)
items.back: 1. Between radar-identified aircraft. 2. Between an aircraft taking off (ID'd within 1 NM of runway) and another radar-identified aircraft. 3. Between radar-identified and non-identified aircraft when climbing/descending through altitude (with 5 conditions). 4. Between radar-identified and non-identified transiting from oceanic/nonradar offshore airspace.

items.front: Five conditions for radar separation between identified and non-identified aircraft (5-5-1b3)
items.back: 1. Adequate radar performance with primary/ASR-9 targets displayed. 2. Flight data shows non-identified aircraft can give adequate return. 3. Airspace not less than: 6 NM (<40 NM from antenna), 10 NM (>=40 NM or narrowband). 4. Radar separation maintained between identified and all observed targets until nonradar separation. 5. If same heading, vector identified aircraft to avoid superimposed targets.

items.front: Edge-of-display distances for mixed identified/non-identified separation (5-5-1b3c)
items.back: <40 NM from antenna: 6 miles. >=40 NM from antenna: 10 miles. Narrowband radar: 10 miles.

items.front: How is radar separation applied between target types? (5-5-2)
items.back: a. Between centers of primary targets (NO touching primary-to-primary or primary-to-beacon-slash). b. Between ends of beacon control slashes. c. Between end of beacon slash and center of primary target. d. All-digital: between centers, NO touching.
Process to ensure correlated/digitized targets DO NOT TOUCH. When used: mandatory traffic advisories AND safety alerts must be issued. Must NOT be used with mosaic radar systems.

items.front: Terminal radar separation minima by system type (5-5-4a-c)
items.back: Single Sensor ASR/DTAS: 3 NM (<40 NM), 5 NM (>=40 NM), for MSSR 3 NM (<60 NM). FUSION: 3 NM for fusion target, 5 NM when ISR displayed. STARS Multi-Sensor: 5 NM.

Below FL600: 5 miles. At or above FL600: 10 miles. Up to/including FL230: 3 miles when all conditions met (in 3 NM area, within 40 NM of radar or 60 NM with MSSR or track-based, reliable targets, facility directive defines area, area on video map, 3 NM target symbol).

items.front: En route wake turbulence separation behind super (5-5-4f1a)
items.back: 5 miles normally. UNLESS super is at/below FL240 AND below 250 knots: then Heavy=6 NM, Large=7 NM, Small=8 NM.

items.front: En route wake turbulence separation behind heavy (5-5-4f1b)
items.back: Heavy behind heavy: 4 miles. Large or small behind heavy: 5 miles.
4 miles when operating directly behind, following a B757 conducting an instrument approach and/or operating within 2,500 feet and less than 500 feet below.
When NOWGT is displayed in aircraft data block: provide 10 miles separation behind the preceding aircraft AND 10 miles separation to the succeeding aircraft.

items.front: 2.5 NM reduced separation on final conditions (5-5-4j)
items.back: FUSION or single sensor slant range (within 40 NM of antenna), established on final within 10 NM of runway. Conditions: (1) wake turbulence per TBL 5-5-2, (2) average runway occupancy time <=50 sec documented, (3) CTRDs operational for quick glance, (4) turnoff points visible from tower.

3 miles increasing to 5+ miles allowed when: (a) diverging routes/courses and/or leader faster; (b) separation constantly increasing; (c) first center controller establishes 5 NM before departure from first center sector; (d) LOA covers procedure on specified routes/sectors.

items.front: Adjacent airspace boundary minima WITHOUT coordination (5-5-10a-b)
items.back: To RADAR airspace: <40 NM=1.5 NM, >=40 NM=2.5 NM, EAS below FL600=2.5 NM, EAS FL600+=5 NM. To NONRADAR airspace: <40 NM=3 NM, >=40 NM=5 NM, EAS below FL600=5 NM, EAS FL600+=10 NM.
The IFR boundary minima do not apply to VFR aircraft receiving Class B, C, or TRSA services. Instead: ensure targets do NOT touch the boundary of adjacent airspace.

items.front: Edge of scope separation when climbing/descending through altitude (5-5-11)
items.back: Apply until nonradar separation established: <40 NM from antenna = 3 NM from edge. >=40 NM = 5 NM from edge. EAS below FL600 = 5 NM. EAS FL600+ = 10 NM.

When using display with previously specified beacon target displacement: add 1 MILE correction factor to applicable minima. Maximum allowable displacement: 1/2 mile (specified by facility air traffic manager).
